methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Aeva Technologies

data.narrative:
Ouster is a publicly traded semiconductor company founded by Angus Pacala. Valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 12 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies across the Chips & Semiconductors sector, spanning both public equities (3 tickers: ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and private ventures (8 firms including PsiQuantum at $7.0B, Etched at $5.0B, and Tenstorrent at $2.7B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. Across the 12-company peer universe, the median valuation stands at $2.2B, with a range spanning $500M (floor) to $7.0B (ceiling). This method anchors the analysis directly to observed transaction and funding valuations in the target's immediate competitive set. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied; while the public semiconductor sector median trades at 24.2x P/S, Ouster's revenue is not on file, precluding reliable multiple application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.8B to $2.5B, centered on $2.2B. Ouster's current $1.5B valuation sits below the peer median by approximately $700M, or 32%. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to private-stage peers (data unavailable), capital efficiency relative to the $500M+ raised, and near-term market positioning within a heterogeneous peer set spanning quantum computing (PsiQuantum) to systems-on-chip designs (Cerebras). Peer dispersion from $500M to $7.0B reflects meaningful stage and technology differentiation.
